title: API Gateway CRUD Application
triggers: client sends requests through an api gateway
backend service reads and writes a database

requiredNodes.label: Client
requiredNodes.description: External caller that initiates CRUD requests.

requiredNodes.label: API Gateway
requiredNodes.description: Single entry point that accepts and forwards API traffic.

requiredNodes.label: Application Service
requiredNodes.description: Business logic service that handles CRUD operations.

requiredNodes.label: Primary Datastore
requiredNodes.description: System of record for application data.

optionalNodes.label: Cache
optionalNodes.description: Read acceleration layer for hot application data.

optionalNodes.label: Audit Log
optionalNodes.description: Append-only store for request and change history.

invariants.rule: Client traffic should traverse the API Gateway before reaching the Application Service.
invariants.rationale: The gateway is the defined ingress boundary for this pattern.

invariants.rule: The Application Service must have at least one read or write edge to the Primary Datastore.
invariants.rationale: A CRUD application needs a persistence boundary for its records.

invariants.rule: The API Gateway should not be modeled as the primary business state store.
invariants.rationale: Gateways mediate traffic rather than serving as the system of record.

antiPatterns.condition: The prompt centers on asynchronous queues, event buses, or background workers.
antiPatterns.rationale: That shape matches a pipeline or event-driven architecture better than a simple CRUD API.

antiPatterns.condition: The prompt focuses on retrieval augmented generation, tool routing, or model reasoning.
antiPatterns.rationale: Those requirements are more specific than a generic CRUD application.

clarifications.question: Does the gateway only route traffic, or should it also enforce auth or rate limits?
clarifications.reason: Ingress responsibilities affect whether this pattern should merge with an auth gateway variant.

clarifications.question: Is there a cache or search layer that should appear alongside the primary datastore?
clarifications.reason: Read optimization and search indexing are common extensions to the base CRUD topology.
